prompt: What does it mean for a logical system to be DECIDABLE?
options: Every formula is either true or false
There is an algorithm that determines, for any formula, whether it is valid
Every valid formula can be proven
hint: Decidability is about a mechanical procedure that always halts with a yes/no answer.
explanation: A system is DECIDABLE if there is an algorithm that, for any formula, halts and correctly answers whether it is valid (or provable). Propositional logic is decidable (truth tables). Option 3 describes completeness, not decidability — a logic can be complete yet undecidable.
